sourceMovie.title: The Hitman's Bodyguard
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: People revisit The Hitman's Bodyguard for the same reason it broke through in 2017: clear stakes, memorable faces, and a comedy rhythm that doesn't stall in the middle. Patrick Hughes keeps the ensemble pointed in one direction. The action and comedy blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: Bad Boys
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: The action energy of The Hitman's Bodyguard is alive in Bad Boys (1995), filtered through a different story but the same emotional register. It earns its place on this list with a funny payoff that rivals the original pick.

recommendations.title: Hitman's Wife's Bodyguard
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: Hitman's Wife's Bodyguard (2021) operates in the same action as The Hitman's Bodyguard, but with its own distinct voice and pacing. The funny payoff will feel satisfyingly familiar without retreading the same ground.

recommendations.title: Smokin' Aces
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: Smokin' Aces (2006) belongs to the same conversation as The Hitman's Bodyguard — both anchor their action in comedy emotion that doesn't let go. The funny undercurrent connects them more than any plot summary would suggest.

recommendations.title: Bad Boys: Ride or Die
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If The Hitman's Bodyguard's action stayed with you, Bad Boys: Ride or Die (2024) delivers the same sensation through an entirely different story. The comedy through-line is the real common thread between them.

recommendations.title: RocknRolla
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: Where The Hitman's Bodyguard excels in action, RocknRolla (2008) doubles down on crime to comparable effect. The tonal similarities run deep enough that watching both back-to-back feels intentional.

recommendations.title: Run All Night
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: Run All Night (2015) is the natural next step after The Hitman's Bodyguard: same action DNA, different perspective. The dark streak and crime elevate it well above a simple genre exercise.

recommendations.title: Colombiana
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: Fans of The Hitman's Bodyguard who respond to its action will find Colombiana (2011) immediately familiar. Both films share a crime sensibility without ever feeling like carbon copies of each other.

recommendations.title: The Jackal
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: There's a reason The Hitman's Bodyguard fans keep landing on The Jackal (1997) — the action is unmistakably related even when the plots diverge. Both films operate on the same emotional frequency, especially in their third acts.

recommendations.title: 16 Blocks
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: 16 Blocks draws from the same action well as The Hitman's Bodyguard, channeling crime in ways that feel unmistakably related. The 2006 film makes a natural companion watch — similar in spirit but distinctly its own.

recommendations.title: Faster
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: Faster (2010) laid groundwork for the tone and themes that The Hitman's Bodyguard later expanded. If The Hitman's Bodyguard resonated for you, this predecessor shows where that emotional DNA began.

faq.question: What should I watch after The Hitman's Bodyguard?
faq.answer: Try Bad Boys, Hitman's Wife's Bodyguard, Smokin' Aces—each shares DNA with The Hitman's Bodyguard while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is The Hitman's Bodyguard?
faq.answer: The Hitman's Bodyguard (2017) is best described as Action, Comedy, Crime, Thriller. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is The Hitman's Bodyguard part of a franchise?
faq.answer: Yes—it's part of the "The Hitman's Bodyguard Collection" storyline. You can enjoy it standalone, but sequels and tie-ins build on characters and continuity established here.
